raw_consumer_statement:
I returned a XXXX items from separate purchases from a merchant XXXX. The first item was for {$640.00} charged on my account for {$1800.00} as part of a larger order dated XX/XX/XXXX. 
I returned this with XXXX tracking XXXX. 
The return was in compliance with the merchants policy The second and third item were part of the same order dated XX/XX/XXXX and were charged to my account as {$750.00} and {$530.00} on that same day. 
Both this items were returned via XXXX  tracking XXXX delivered on XX/XX/XXXX. Again this was fully compliant with the merchants return policy. 

The merchant did not refund me so I submitted a dispute with Citi bank. They refused to open the dispute or credit me. They broke the law. I submitted the dispute in writing within the timeframe that the Fair credit act stipulates. 

The case is simple. The merchant is in possession of the returned items and I am missing credit totaling {$1900.00} which Citi refuses to cooperate. 

Citi should be investigated because they also have charged me interest in the dispute process which is AGAINST THE LAW. 

Furthermore Citi had blatantly sent me documentation that is PROVABLY FALSE. They said the merchant credited my account. I can prove via my bank statement that is never happened. Yet Citi continues to disregard its customers and the law.
